mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses corporate revenue growth and margin expansion, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE remains above 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer spending across durable and non-durable sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum that translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns during early-cycle recovery phases, with directional accuracy below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and a declining unemployment rate creates a favorable liquidity and earnings environment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal generates negative 20-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 0.5 percent, aggregate demand stagnation forces corporate earnings revisions downward, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 10-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation combined with constrained policy rates signals emerging stagflationary pressures that compress real discount rates and equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint inflation-rate signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with directional accuracy below 45 percent during inflationary shock periods.
